mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) pressures central banks to tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when CPI exceeds 320.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through stronger corporate earnings.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10) or the combined signal's IC falls below 0.07.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 5%) indicates weak labor markets, suppressing consumer spending and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Positive employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10) or the combined signal's IC falls below 0.07.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and HOUST fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10) or the combined signal's IC falls below 0.07.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4%) reduces liquidity and raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during policy rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing activity (HOUST) indicate elevated demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased corporate revenue.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and HOUST fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10) or the combined signal's IC falls below 0.07.
